summary: DHH reflects on the rapid growth and momentum of Ruby on Rails, noting that the framework has taken on a life of its own. He marvels at the constant stream of announcements and developments coming from both expected and unexpected sources. The post serves as a brief celebration of how far Rails has come in just one year, positioning it as a technology that has truly arrived.
key_points: Rails had developed momentum beyond DHH's direct control, with the community driving growth independently
New announcements and developments were arriving daily from diverse and unexpected sources
DHH frames Rails as having definitively 'arrived' as a mainstream technology in just one year
money_quotes: The beast has certainly taken on a life of its own.
Ruby on Rails has really arrived.
I can't believe how far we've come in just a year.
key_insight: By late 2005, Ruby on Rails had crossed the threshold from a niche framework to a self-sustaining ecosystem with its own momentum and community-driven growth.
